text:
REGIME — Risk-off, commodities led, financials and tech hammered, breadth weak.

INDICES — VIX 31.0, elevated and rising. Today's return ranking: SPY -1.71%, DIA -1.72%, IWM -1.75%, QQQ -1.95%. Large caps outperformed small caps marginally. VIXY surged +7.72%, the single most notable cross-asset move, confirming broad fear acceleration.

SECTORS — XLE +1.69%, XLP +0.79%, XLU +0.57%, XLB -0.37%, XLRE -0.69%, XLI -1.28%, XLC -1.63%, XLV -1.70%, XLK -1.95%, XLF -2.53%, XLY -2.89%. Top industry ETFs: USO +5.92%, SLV +4.39%. Bottom industry ETFs: ARKG -5.66%, MSOS -8.12%.

THE 7s — Old Metals +3.07%, New Metals +2.22%, Energy +1.47%, Power +0.92%, Old Staples +0.81%, Buildout -0.30%, Fab -0.43%, Freight -0.62%, Industrial -0.97%, Health -1.29%, Housing -1.65%, Defense -1.76%, Medtech -2.03%, New Staples -2.13%, Semi -2.16%, Insurance -2.18%, Toll Road -2.45%, Cloud -2.94%, Bank -3.05%, Software -3.67%. Strongest ticker in Old Metals: SQM +6.89%. Weakest in Software basket, consistent with WCLD -4.39% and WGMI -5.10% drag.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed at 48.3, Neutral. Top 3 movers: SQM +6.89% (Old Metals), USO +5.92% (Energy), MSOS -8.12% (Industries). 20d momentum best sector: XLE +12.61%; worst: XLV -10.21%. Best basket 20d: Energy +14.96%; worst: Housing -15.59%. Brent crude surging past $100 directly drove XLE +1.69% and crushed consumer discretionary and tech via inflation-reignition fears.

WATCH — SQM +6.89%, vol spike 3.4x, momentum divergence against Old Metals 20d -22.60% suggests potential reversal or isolated catalyst worth confirming. META -3.99%, vol spike 2.0x, 20d trend deteriorating inside weak QQQ tape. MSOS -8.12%, vol 2.1x, multi-timeframe breakdown warrants downside follow-through monitoring.
